top_categories.workout_context: 57 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic VeloTurk Gran Fondo Cesme's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
VeloTurk Gran Fondo Cesme's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 57 miles.
60ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At VeloTurk Gran Fondo Cesme, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of VeloTurk Gran Fondo Cesme. Train it until it feels boring.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Base fitness for VeloTurk Gran Fondo Cesme. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At VeloTurk Gran Fondo Cesme, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.heat: April in Cesme, Izmir, Turkey can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before VeloTurk Gran Fondo Cesme. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for VeloTurk Gran Fondo Cesme’s 57 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at VeloTurk Gran Fondo Cesme requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 57 miles of mixed terrain in Cesme, Izmir, Turkey.
